sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to products, as defined in Section 2 below, and offered for direct consumption, including for catering purposes or for repacking if required. The products covered by this standard include, but are not limited to onions, garlic, mango, radish, ginger, beetroot, royal plum, peppers, hearts of palm, cabbage, lettuce, lemons, baby corn (young corn) , and green mustard ( Brassica juncea ssp). It does not apply to the product when indicated as being intended for further processing. This standard does not cover pickled cucumbers, kimchi, table olives, sauerkraut, chutneys and relishes.

sections.title: Product definition
sections.retrieval_text:
DESCRIPTION > Product definition
number=2.1
level=2
Pickled fruits and vegetables is the product:
(a) prepared from sound, clean and edible fruits and/or vegetables, with or without seeds, spices, aromatic herbs and/or condiments;
(b) processed or treated to produce an acid or acidified product preserved through natural fermentation or acidulants. Depending on the type, appropriate ingredients are added in order to ensure preservation and quality of the product;
(c) processed in an appropriate manner, before or after being hermetically sealed in a container, so as to ensure the quality and safety as well as to prevent spoilage; and/or
(d) packed with or without a suitable liquid packing medium (e.g. oil, brine or acidic media such as vinegar) as specified in Section 3.1.2, with ingredients appropriate to the type and variety of pickled product, to ensure an equilibrium pH of less than 4.6.

sections.title: Packing media
sections.text:
For pickled fruits, in accordance with the Guidelines for Packing Media for Canned Fruits (CXG 51-2003). For pickled vegetables, in accordance with the following provisions:
(a) Basic ingredients Water, and if necessary, salt or oil or acidic media such as vinegar.
(b) Optional ingredients Packing media for pickled vegetables may contain ingredients subject to labelling requirements of Section 8 and may include, but is not limited to:
(1) foodstuff with sweetening properties such as sugars (including syrups) as defined in the Standard for Sugars (CXS 212-1999), honey as defined in the Standard for Honey (CXS 12-1981) or juices and/or nectars as defined in the General Standard for Fruit Juices and Nectars (CXS 247-2005) and;

sections.title: Definition of defects
sections.text:
(a) Blemishes – means any characteristic including, but not limited to, bruises, scab s, and dark 
discolouration, which adversely affects the overall appearance of the product.  
(b) Harmless extraneous material – means any vegetable part (such as, but not limited to, a leaf or portion 
thereof, or a stem) that does not pose any hazard to human health but affects the overall appearance 
of the final product.

sections.title: FOOD ADDITIVES
sections.text:
Acidity regulators, antifoaming agents, antioxidants, colours, colour retention agents , firming agents, flavour 
enhancers, preservatives, sequestrants, stabilizers and sweeteners used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 
of the General Standard of Food Additives (CXS 192-1995)6 in the food category in which the individual pickled 
fruit or vegetable fall into (i.e.  one of the following categories: 04.1.2.3, 04.1.2.10, 04.2.2.3, and 04.2.2.7) or 
listed in Table 3 of the General Standard of Food Additives6 are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this 
standard.

sections.title: Lot acceptance
sections.text:
A lot should be considered as meeting the applicable quality requirements referred to in Section 3.2 when:  
(a) for those requirements which are not based on averages, the number of “defectives”, as defined in 
Section 3.3, does not exceed the acceptance number  (c) of the appropriate sampling plan with an 
acceptable quality limit (AQL) of 6.5; and 
(b) the requirements, which are based on sample averages, are complied with.

(a) for those requirements which are not based on averages, the number of “defectives”, as defined in 
Section 3.3, does not exceed the acceptance number  (c) of the appropriate sampling plan with an 
acceptable quality limit (AQL) of 6.5; and 
(b) the requirements, which are based on sample averages, are complied with.

sections.title: Minimum fill
sections.text:
The container should be well filled with the product (including packing medium when appropriate) which should 
occupy not less than 90 percent (minus any necessary head space according to good manufacturing practices) 
of the water capacity of the container. The water capacity of the container is the volume of distilled water at 
20 oC which the sealed container will hold when completely filled.

sections.title: Minimum drained weight
sections.text: The drained weight of the product should be not less than the following percentages, calculated on the basis of the weight of distilled water at 20 oC which the sealed container will hold when completely filled.ii (a) Whole and halves style should not be less than 40 percent of the net weight; (b) Pieces style and other styles should not be less than 50 percent of the net weight (except for pickled red cabbage should not be less than 45 percent of the net weight).

classification.sentence:
For pickled vegetables, in accordance with the following provisions:
(a) Basic ingredients Water, and if necessary, salt or oil or acidic media such as vinegar.
(b) Optional ingredients Packing media for pickled vegetables may contain ingredients subject to labelling requirements of Section 8 and may include, but is not limited to:
(1) foodstuff with sweetening properties such as sugars (including syrups) as defined in the Standard for Sugars (CXS 212-1999), honey as defined in the Standard for Honey (CXS 12-1981) or juices and/or nectars as defined in the General Standard for Fruit Juices and Nectars (CXS 247-2005) and;
classification.function: specifying provisions for pickled vegetables ingredients

pages.summary: Página contém o título principal do documento como nível 1, com texto explicativo e código no cabeçalho. Nenhuma seção adicional detectada.

A página traz duas seções principais de nível 1 '2022 Amendments' e '2023 Amendments', com texto explicativo associado a cada uma. Não há título principal do documento visível nesta página. O cabeçalho contém código e paginação. As seções não estão em continuidade com a página anterior nem continuam na próxima.

A página apresenta três seções principais de nível 1: 'SCOPE', 'DESCRIPTION' e 'ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS'. 'DESCRIPTION' e 'ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS' possuem múltiplos níveis de subseções mapeados corretamente. O texto interno foi associado a seus respectivos nós. A seção 3.1.2 'Packing media' e sua seção superior 3.1 e seção 3 continuam na próxima página, evidenciado por 'continues_on_next_page=true'. Não há título principal do documento nesta página visível e o cabeçalho contém código e paginação.

A página apresenta a continuação da seção 3 com múltiplas subseções de níveis 2, 3 e 4. O conteúdo está corretamente associado aos títulos numerados. A seção 3 é continuação da página anterior (is_continuation_from_previous_page=true) e continua na próxima página (continues_on_next_page=true). Não há título principal do documento visível, e o cabeçalho exibe código e paginação.

A página apresenta continuação da seção 3.4 com texto detalhado, seguida por as seções 4, 5, 6 e 7 com suas subseções, onde a hierarquia é bem estabelecida e o texto interno é adequadamente associado aos nós. A seção 3.4 continua da página anterior e prossegue para a próxima. Não há título principal do documento visível. Cabeçalho com código e paginação está presente.

A página apresenta hierarquia clara com seções numeradas e títulos em caixa alta. A seção 7.1.4 e suas subseções são continuadas da página anterior. A seção 8 é detalhada com subseções e subsubseções. A seção 9 e as NOTAS são incluídas com texto explicativo. O cabeçalho e a paginação são identificados. Texto interno foi associado adequadamente e continuidade marcada para a seção 7.1.4 e notas.

A página contém uma seção única "REFERENCES" que é uma continuação da seção precedente da página anterior. Contém referências bibliográficas numeradas. Não há novos títulos numerados ou subtítulos nesta página. O cabeçalho indica o código do documento e o número da página.
